assessmentItems.name: Assessment 1: Individual Assignment
assessmentItems.dueDate: Part 1 Week 3 by 9pm Friday; Part 2 Week 5 by 9pm Friday; Part 3 Week 7 by 9pm Friday
assessmentItems.format: Each part will be started by students as an in-class activity

assessmentItems.name: Assessment 2: Group Project
assessmentItems.format: working in small teams to apply quantitative analysis and modelling techniques

assessmentItems.name: Assessment 3: Final Exam
assessmentItems.format: 20 short answer questions that cover content from Weeks 5 to 10

learningObjectives: Students will demonstrate a solid understanding of quantitative analysis in financial markets, including the different trading strategies employed and the importance of risk management.
Students will be able to apply statistical analysis and modelling techniques to derive insights from financial data, enabling them to develop and evaluate trading strategies.

Students will develop practical skills in back testing and performance evaluation, including the ability to design, conduct, and interpret back tests to assess the performance of trading strategies. They will also learn how to evaluate and optimise trading strategies based on the results obtained.
Students will develop critical thinking skills in assessing market microstructure, analysing order book dynamics, and understanding the impact of market liquidity on trading strategies.
